title: build arguments behave as empty in podman build
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When using build arguments they appear to be treated as empty when I use `podman build`.  This seems to happen whether I am giving them default values or setting them on command line with `--build-arg`.  Or whether I am using them in an `ENV` or a `RUN`.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Consider the following test build file:
```docker
ARG PY_MAJOR=3
ARG PY_MINOR=6

FROM registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi-minimal:8.3

ENV PIPENV_PYTHON="${PY_MAJOR}${PY_MINOR}"

RUN echo $PIPENV_PYTHON
RUN echo "${PY_MAJOR}${PY_MINOR}"
```

Build using defaults:

```sh
$ podman build -t podman:test --no-cache .
STEP 1: FROM registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi-minimal:8.3
STEP 2: ENV PIPENV_PYTHON="${PY_MAJOR}${PY_MINOR}"
--> 67a41e83882
STEP 3: RUN echo $PIPENV_PYTHON

--> 5585cae8d20
STEP 4: RUN echo "${PY_MAJOR}${PY_MINOR}"

STEP 5: COMMIT podman:test
--> 0d91970225a
0d91970225ae9c07c90e318ab51ab52fe39d27719f73064bed29c378ee516c2a
```

build using `--build-arg`

```sh
[eje@localhost ray-ubi]$ podman build -t podman:test --build-arg PY_MAJOR=3 --build-arg PY_MINOR=6 --no-cache .
STEP 1: FROM registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi-minimal:8.3
STEP 2: ENV PIPENV_PYTHON="${PY_MAJOR}${PY_MINOR}"
--> 7fc622ce164
STEP 3: RUN echo $PIPENV_PYTHON

--> 9b15fa0591f
STEP 4: RUN echo "${PY_MAJOR}${PY_MINOR}"

STEP 5: COMMIT podman:test
--> 521d0c1f59f
521d0c1f59f66b5185d29016a705a593f583ce612e14d822936c96c4f9dbe2c5
```

**Describe the results you received:**

build arguments behave as if they are empty (see example builds above)

**Describe the results you expected:**

In the example above would expect the `RUN echo ...` commands to both print `36`

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Running on fedora 33, podman installed via `dnf install podman`

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.15.5
Built:        Tue Dec  8 07:37:50 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

Yes
(running podman 2.2.1)

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: Can you upgrade to Podman 3.0 and retry?
Will try to install 3.0, report back

@mheon   I installed 3.0.1-dev  via COPR,  it didn't seem to work either:

(that seems like a weird build date - 2018?)

I attempted the build described above with Docker and got the same results as Podman/Buildah.

When I moved the ARG definitions after the from

```
cat /tmp/test/Containerfile 
FROM registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi-minimal:8.3
ARG PY_MAJOR=3
ARG PY_MINOR=6

ENV PIPENV_PYTHON="${PY_MAJOR}${PY_MINOR}"

RUN echo $PIPENV_PYTHON
RUN echo "${PY_MAJOR}${PY_MINOR}"
```
Docker, Podman and Buildah worked the same.

```
# podman build -t podman:test --build-arg PY_MAJOR=3 --build-arg PY_MINOR=6 --no-cache -f /tmp/test/Containerfile /tmp/test/
STEP 1: FROM registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi-minimal:8.3
Getting image source signatures
Checking if image destination supports signatures
Copying blob a6b97b4963f5 done  
Copying blob 13948a011eec done  
Copying config 7331d26c1f done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2: ARG PY_MAJOR=3
--> ea825343d81
STEP 3: ARG PY_MINOR=6
--> cd09b5b30d7
STEP 4: ENV PIPENV_PYTHON="${PY_MAJOR}${PY_MINOR}"
--> 98a306fabb6
STEP 5: RUN echo $PIPENV_PYTHON
36
--> 2527f5f25a3
STEP 6: RUN echo "${PY_MAJOR}${PY_MINOR}"
36
STEP 7: COMMIT podman:test
--> 2dcc2b06751
2dcc2b06751fa1f9f7e4ef31127608a0d552fb01ceb5a20dd2960507e88c20e4
```

Here is the output of Docker with the initial Containerfile
```
# docker -v
Docker version 20.10.3, build 48d30b5
# docker build -t podman:test --build-arg PY_MAJOR=3 --build-arg PY_MINOR=6 --no-cache -f /tmp/test/Containerfile /tmp/test/
Sending build context to Docker daemon  2.048kB
Step 1/6 : ARG PY_MAJOR=3
Step 2/6 : ARG PY_MINOR=6
Step 3/6 : FROM registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi-minimal:8.3
 ---> 7331d26c1fdf
Step 4/6 : ENV PIPENV_PYTHON="${PY_MAJOR}${PY_MINOR}"
 ---> Running in 4d0070bb67c5
Removing intermediate container 4d0070bb67c5
 ---> 17ebf4c78fad
Step 5/6 : RUN echo $PIPENV_PYTHON
 ---> Running in de8b74b9f89b

Removing intermediate container de8b74b9f89b
 ---> 465510a24ccc
Step 6/6 : RUN echo "${PY_MAJOR}${PY_MINOR}"
 ---> Running in ba168a7c9c67

Removing intermediate container ba168a7c9c67
 ---> 8c69bdf233fd
Successfully built 8c69bdf233fd
Successfully tagged podman:test
```
I don't believe this is a bug. Reopen if I am missing something?
Thanks! I misinterpreted the scoping rules - you can define ARG before FROM but after that FROM they go out of scope

@rhatdan is spot on, this is a scoping issue with the ARG and FROM statement.  Once the Containerfile processing hits a FROM, it wipes out any ARG statements prior.    In a Containerfile like this one, it doesn't seem to make intuitive sense, but it is very useful when you have multiple FROM statements in a Containerfile.
@TomSweeneyRedHat thanks!   At the risk of going off on a tangent, I'd be extremely interested to see an example of a docker-file where nuking the ARG scope at each FROM is useful.

@erikerlandson couple of thoughts.  The first is from this [commit](https://github.com/containers/buildah/commit/7535471ff6950fbff24073ef91c6be89e697823a) where the Containerfile contained:

```

FROM alpine
ARG SECRET
RUN echo $SECRET

FROM alpine
RUN echo "$SECRET" > test_file
```

In it, the build process knows the value of the secret, but the final image has no clue.  Ala:

```
# buildah bud --build-arg SECRET=tomsecret -t tom -f ~/Dockerfile.multiargfrom .
STEP 1: FROM alpine
STEP 2: ARG SECRET
STEP 3: RUN echo $SECRET
tomsecret
STEP 4: FROM alpine
STEP 5: RUN echo "$SECRET" > test_file
STEP 6: COMMIT tom
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 1119ff37d4a9 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob d03ce8cee502 done  
Copying config bdbeaf4317 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> bdbeaf43172
bdbeaf431723baf8e8267f0b04aa1276066f3aaecced72be30d45a83b5b5fc3e

# buildah from tom
tom-working-container

# buildah run tom-working-container cat test_file

(An empty line was printed)
```

It also would be useful as shown in this [post](https://levelup.gitconnected.com/docker-multi-stage-builds-and-copy-from-other-images-3bf1a2b095e0) by Mike Nothigher.  In his Dockerfile he pushed in the MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD for his db so he could use the db, but due to the multi FROM's it wasn't in his final image.  

HTH
@TomSweeneyRedHat  I love that example, thank you!
